topic: excessive fermentation temperature and burnt aromas
trigger_keywords: aromas quemados en un vino tinto

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A red-wine fermentation becomes excessively hot.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Excess heat stresses or inhibits yeast and can accelerate unwanted chemical reactions while driving off desirable volatile aromas.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The wine can lose fresh fruit expression and develop coarse cooked or burnt-smelling characters.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Excessively high fermentation temperature can damage aroma quality and contribute cooked or burnt characters.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
